integrity.description: The outputs were generated by executing the script, not by manual GUI actions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the execution of the script (e.g., using 'gretlcli') or the Gretl GUI executing the script file, rather than point-and-click operations to save each file individually.

integrity.description: The script dynamically generates the report rather than hardcoding the expected values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The .inp script uses Gretl accessors (like $coeff, $rsq) or output redirection (outfile) to write the results, rather than explicitly echoing the numbers '83.416', '10.2096', etc., into the text file.

integrity.description: The generated plots are genuine Gretl/gnuplot outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter plot and histogram exhibit standard gnuplot/Gretl styling and formatting, rather than being generic placeholder images or screenshots taken from the web.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OLS regression of food_exp on income yields an intercept of approximately 83.416, a slope of 10.210, and an R-squared of 0.385. The Jarque-Bera normality test on the residuals produces a statistic of approximately 0.0633 and a p-value of 0.9688. The dataset contains 40 observations.
